outlook.headline: Advanced-node demand is broadening into N2 and 18A
outlook.summary: Advanced-node activity looks to be strengthening over the next 1-2 quarters, with 2nm/N2 ramps, 18A yield progress, and broader capacity builds showing up across foundry, equipment, EDA, and materials. The biggest beneficiaries are ASML, KLA, Cadence, Entegris, TSMC, and Intel, while the main laggards are names exposed to softer mature-node spend like Lam and Veeco. The mention trend is also supportive at 1.7x vs trailing average with +5% momentum, suggesting attention is still building rather than fading.

drivers.label: N2/2nm ramps are actively pulling demand
drivers.detail: Customers are building capacity across 2nm/N2 and related leading-edge nodes, supporting near-term tool, material, and design spend.

drivers.label: Tight supply and higher complexity increase content per wafer
drivers.detail: Sub-2nm and GAA ramps are described as tight or supply-constrained, which typically lifts demand for process-control, materials, and equipment content.

drivers.label: Design activity remains strong at advanced nodes
drivers.detail: AI-driven tapeouts, new node wins, and first-to-market IP point to continued adoption of advanced-node solutions.

drivers.label: Execution is improving at the leading edge
drivers.detail: Better 18A yields and ongoing N2 ramp progress suggest the node transition is becoming more credible, even if ramp costs remain a drag.

drivers.label: Mature-node softness is the main offset
drivers.detail: Comments around softer or flattish mature-node spending indicate the improvement is not uniform across the broader semiconductor stack.

risks: 2nm/18A ramp costs or yield issues could slow adoption and delay spending.
A softer mature-node or China spending backdrop could cap upside for select equipment suppliers.
If AI-related design wins fail to convert into wafer starts, the current demand broadening could stall.
